title: Charcot and the psychology of hysteria, with special reference to a never published final case history.
abstract: Jean-Martin Charcot is perhaps best remembered for his contributions to organic neurology. However, his pursuit of hysteria, the most prevalent diagnosis in his hospital clinic, yielded no anatomical lesion to account for hysteria's plethora of somatic disorders assumed due to a purely functional or
journal: Journal of the history of the neurosciences
